expectations.text: [phase_contract][blocking] Case focus is explicitly covered: REPORT_STATE and resume semantics remain stable
expectations.evidence: result.md explicitly covers the case focus: it includes a dedicated section "REPORT_STATE definition and expected user interaction (core benchmark focus)" describing REPORT_STATE values (FINAL_EXISTS, DRAFT_EXISTS, CONTEXT_ONLY, FRESH) and "After user choice" semantics including the required call to `scripts/apply_user_choice.sh` and routing (full_regenerate → next phase0; smart_refresh → next phase2; reuse/resume → continue). It then states: "Focus explicitly covered: REPORT_STATE and resume semantics remain stable — Pass."

expectations.text: [phase_contract][blocking] Output aligns with primary phase phase0
expectations.evidence: result.md is organized as a "Phase 0 Contract Check (Alignment to phase0)" and lists phase0 required behaviors and artifacts (runtime setup, report_state classification, normalization, policies) plus required output artifacts under "What phase0 is required to do". It then asserts "Output aligns with primary phase phase0 — Pass (phase0 contract alignment is explicit)."
